input.prompt:
I'm building a photo viewer page in my .NET MAUI app targeting .NET 10. I want
the image to fill the entire screen edge-to-edge, going behind the status bar
and navigation bar. But my image still has padding around it even though I set
SafeAreaEdges="None" on the ContentPage. What am I missing?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a photo viewer page in my .NET MAUI app targeting .NET 10. I want
the image to fill the entire screen edge-to-edge, going behind the status bar
and navigation bar. But my image still has padding around it even though I set
SafeAreaEdges="None" on the ContentPage. What am I missing?

data.content:
`SafeAreaEdges` isn't a standard .NET MAUI property — that's likely why it's having no effect. Here's what you actually need:

### 1. iOS — Ignore safe area insets
Set `IgnoreSafeArea="True"` on the **layout**, not the page:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             Shell.NavBarIsVisible="False">
    <Grid IgnoreSafeArea="True">
        <Image Source="photo.jpg"
               Aspect="AspectFill" />
    </Grid>
</ContentPage>
```

### 2. Android — Enable edge-to-edge
Android doesn't go behind system bars by default (pre-Android 15). In your `MainActivity.cs`:

```csharp
protected override void OnCreate(Bundle? savedInstanceState)
{
    base.OnCreate(savedInstanceState);

    if (Window != null)
    {
        Window.SetStatusBarColor(Android.Graphics.Color.Transparent);
        Window.SetNavigationBarColor(Android.Graphics.Color.Transparent);

        WindowCompat.SetDecorFitsSystemWindows(Window, false);
    }
}
```

> **Note:** On Android 15+ (API 35), edge-to-edge is enforced automatically, so the above is mainly needed for older targets.

### 3. Hide app chrome
Make sure Shell/NavigationPage bars aren't adding space:

```xml
Shell.NavBarIsVisible="False"
Shell.TabBarIsVisible="False"
```

The combination of `IgnoreSafeArea="True"` on the layout + transparent system bars on Android should give you the full bleed you're after.
data.reasoningText:
The user is working on a .NET MAUI app targeting .NET 10 and wants an image to go edge-to-edge (behind status bar and navigation bar). They've tried setting `SafeAreaEdges="None"` on the ContentPage but the image still has padding.

This is a common .NET MAUI question. Let me think about what's needed:

1. In .NET MAUI, `IgnoreSafeArea` is the property on views/layouts, not `SafeAreaEdges` on ContentPage.
2. On iOS, you need to handle safe area insets. The way to do this in MAUI is to set `IgnoreSafeArea="True"` on the layout or view.


3. On Android, handling edge-to-edge requires enabling immersive mode for the system bars. In .NET 10, there might be built-in support for this, but I need to clarify whether they're using platform-specific code or if MAUI has a unified approach for this.

Actually, let me reconsider the specifics here. `SafeAreaEdges` isn't a standard .NET MAUI property—that sounds like either a Xamarin.Forms concept or possibly a new .NET 10 feature I'm not fully aware of. For iOS, the way to ignore safe areas is setting `IgnoreSafeArea="True"` on the root layout. On Android, you'd typically need platform-specific code using `WindowCompat.SetDecorFitsSystemWindows(window, false)` to achieve edge-to-edge rendering. The real question is whether `SafeAreaEdges` is something they're trying to use that doesn't actually exist in their current MAUI version, or if it's a newer API I should account for. I'm realizing .NET 10 is still quite new, and the edge-to-edge behavior has evolved significantly—especially with Android 15's enforcement. The user might be confusing properties from older Xamarin.Forms APIs or mixing up what actually exists in .NET MAUI. I should clarify that `SafeAreaEdges` isn't a real property and focus on what actually works: `IgnoreSafeArea` on the layout itself for iOS, and the platform-specific Android setup with `WindowCompat.SetDecorFitsSystemWindows` for true edge-to-edge rendering.
